title: taraxacum perincisum
intro: Taraxacum perincisum, also known as the perennial dandelion, is a subspecies of the common dandelion (Taraxacum officinale) characterized by its perennial growth habit and larger leaves. The roots, leaves, and flowers of the perennial dandelion have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, liver problems, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum perincisum include its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as supporting kidney function, reducing inflammation, and protecting against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum perincisum include teas, tinctures, and infusions made from the roots and leaves, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
